nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Module root for the agentic (coding-assistant) tool-call domain; re-exports the shared types and the command renderer consumed by the daemon pipeline, brain enrichment, and MCP query tools.
nodes.languageNotes: Rust module barrel using `pub use` to flatten render and types into the agentic namespace.

nodes.name: render.rs
nodes.summary: Renders an agentic tool call into a single human-readable command string, dispatching on the tool name (Bash, Read, Edit, Grep, Agent, etc.) to extract the salient field from the tool input JSON.

nodes.name: render_command
nodes.summary: Maps a tool name plus its JSON input to a concise command-line representation, falling back to the bare tool name for unrecognized tools.

nodes.name: types.rs
nodes.summary: Defines the core agentic data model: the AgenticToolCall record plus the Harness, AgenticStatus, and TokenUsage enums/structs that flow from ingesters through enrichment into the knowledge graph.
nodes.languageNotes: Rust enums with #[serde]-friendly `as_db_str` helpers that map variants to stable database string values.

nodes.name: Harness
nodes.summary: Enum identifying the coding-assistant harness (ClaudeCode, Opencode, Codex, Unknown) that produced a tool call, with helpers to map to DB strings and source basenames.

nodes.name: AgenticToolCall
nodes.summary: Rich record capturing a single coding-assistant tool invocation: session lineage, harness/model/provider, command, output, token usage, cost, git state, and redaction count.

nodes.name: config.rs
nodes.summary: Defines the full Hippo configuration tree (inference, models, daemon, brain, storage, browser, telemetry, github, watchdog, opencode, codex, reaper) plus the redaction config, with serde defaults and TOML loaders.
nodes.languageNotes: Rust config pattern: every field has a serde `default = "..."` free function and a `Default` impl per section, so a sparse TOML file still deserializes into a complete config.

nodes.name: HippoConfig
nodes.summary: Root configuration struct aggregating every subsystem config section; provides load/load_default plus derived path accessors (db_path, socket_path, log_path, fallback_dir).

nodes.name: load
nodes.summary: Loads and deserializes a HippoConfig from a TOML file path, applying serde defaults for any missing sections or fields.

nodes.name: RedactConfig
nodes.summary: Holds the ordered list of redaction patterns; loads from a TOML file or falls back to a built-in default pattern set.

nodes.name: builtin
nodes.summary: Returns the built-in default redaction pattern set used when no redact.toml is present.

nodes.name: events.rs
nodes.summary: Defines the EventEnvelope and the EventPayload variant tree (Shell, FsChange, IdeAction, Browser, AgenticToolCall, Raw) that constitute the daemon's capture wire format, re-exporting the shared primitives.
nodes.languageNotes: Adjacently-tagged serde enum (EventPayload) lets a single envelope carry heterogeneous capture payloads while staying self-describing on the wire.

nodes.name: EventEnvelope
nodes.summary: Top-level capture record wrapping any payload with an envelope id, producer version, timestamp, and optional probe tag; includes a `shell` convenience constructor.

nodes.name: ShellEvent
nodes.summary: Captured shell command event with session id, command, exit code, duration, cwd, captured stdout/stderr, env snapshot, git state, and redaction count.

nodes.name: gh_annotations.rs
nodes.summary: Parses GitHub Actions check-run annotation messages into structured (tool, rule_id) pairs, recognizing common linter/formatter output shapes for workflow enrichment.

nodes.name: parse
nodes.summary: Parses a job name and annotation message into an optional ParsedAnnotation, matching known tool prefixes and rule-id patterns.

nodes.name: lib.rs
nodes.summary: Crate root for hippo-core; declares and re-exports the shared library modules (agentic, config, events, gh_annotations, primitives, protocol, redaction, storage) used by the daemon and brain.
nodes.languageNotes: Rust crate root that is purely a set of `pub mod` declarations exposing the shared core API.

nodes.name: primitives.rs
nodes.summary: Defines small shared value types (CapturedOutput for truncated stream capture, GitState for repo/branch/commit/dirty status) reused across event payloads.

nodes.name: CapturedOutput
nodes.summary: Represents captured stdout/stderr content with a truncation flag and the original byte length before truncation.

nodes.name: GitState
nodes.summary: Snapshot of the working-tree git context: repository, branch, commit, and dirty flag.

nodes.name: protocol.rs
nodes.summary: Defines the Unix-socket wire protocol between the CLI and daemon: the DaemonRequest/DaemonResponse enums plus the info structs (status, sessions, events, entities, query hits) carried in responses.
nodes.languageNotes: Request/response enum pair forms a typed RPC contract serialized over the daemon's length-prefixed Unix socket.

nodes.name: DaemonRequest
nodes.summary: Enum of all commands the CLI can send to the daemon over the Unix socket (ingest event, get status/sessions/events/entities, raw query, register watch SHA, heartbeat, shutdown).

nodes.name: DaemonResponse
nodes.summary: Enum of all reply variants the daemon returns to the CLI (ack, status, sessions, events, entities, query result, error).

nodes.name: StatusInfo
nodes.summary: Daemon health/status snapshot: version, uptime, today's event/session counts, queue depth, drop count, reachability flags, DB size, and pending fallback files.

nodes.name: redaction.rs
nodes.summary: Implements the secret-redaction engine: compiles configured regex patterns into a RegexSet and replaces matched secrets in captured text before storage, returning a count and per-pattern hits.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a compiled regex RegexSet for fast multi-pattern matching so every captured string is scrubbed in a single pass.

nodes.name: RedactionEngine
nodes.summary: Holds the compiled RegexSet plus per-pattern regexes and names; constructed from config, a config path, or the built-in pattern set.

nodes.name: redact
nodes.summary: Scans an input string against all configured patterns, replacing each match with its replacement and returning the redacted text plus a hit count.

nodes.name: storage.rs
nodes.summary: The SQLite persistence layer for hippo-core: opens and migrates the database (embedding schema.sql), and provides all read/write functions for sessions, events, browser events, entities, status, and the fallback-file recovery path.
nodes.languageNotes: Embeds schema.sql via include_str! and runs an idempotent forward-migration ladder (add_column_if_missing / table_exists) so any older on-disk schema upgrades to the expected version on open.

nodes.name: open_db
nodes.summary: Opens the SQLite database at a path, sets WAL/foreign-keys/busy-timeout pragmas, applies schema.sql, and runs the full version-migration ladder to bring the schema to EXPECTED_VERSION.

nodes.name: insert_event_at
nodes.summary: Inserts a captured event at an explicit timestamp, resolving the source kind, env snapshot, envelope id, and probe tag, and updating source-health bookkeeping.

nodes.name: insert_browser_event
nodes.summary: Persists a browser capture event with its timestamp, envelope id, and probe tag into the browser_events table.

nodes.name: get_status
nodes.summary: Builds a StatusInfo snapshot by querying counts, queue depth, DB size, and reachability bookkeeping for the daemon status command.

nodes.name: recover_fallback_files
nodes.summary: Replays JSONL fallback files written when the DB was unavailable, re-inserting their envelopes and removing successfully recovered files.

nodes.name: agentic_envelope.rs
nodes.summary: Integration tests that round-trip an AgenticToolCall through an adjacently-tagged EventEnvelope and verify started_at can diverge from the envelope timestamp.

nodes.name: agentic_render.rs
nodes.summary: Table-driven tests asserting render_command produces the expected concise string for every supported agentic tool (Bash, Read, Edit, Grep, Agent, Skill, WebFetch, etc.) and falls back for unknown tools.

nodes.name: agentic_types.rs
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the agentic types: JSON round-tripping of AgenticToolCall, Harness Unknown handling, and the as_db_str / source_basename mappings.

nodes.name: schema_v5_migration.rs
nodes.summary: Migration test seeding a v4 database fixture and asserting open_db upgrades it to the latest schema with the workflow tables present.

nodes.name: schema_v6_migration.rs
nodes.summary: Migration test seeding a v5 fixture and verifying upgrade to latest plus FTS5 index and trigger behavior (insert/update/delete keep the knowledge_nodes FTS table in sync).

nodes.name: schema_v7_migration.rs
nodes.summary: Migration test seeding a v6 fixture and asserting the upgrade adds source_kind and tool_name columns, backfills existing rows to source_kind 'shell', and lands fresh DBs at v9.

nodes.name: watchlist.rs
nodes.summary: Tests the storage watchlist submodule: upsert-then-resolve, terminal marking of an unknown SHA returning false, and expired entries being inactive.

nodes.name: workflow_store.rs
nodes.summary: Tests the storage workflow_store submodule: upserting workflow runs, parsing annotations on insert, and idempotent enrichment enqueueing.

nodes.name: schema.sql
nodes.summary: Canonical SQLite schema for the hippo database (34 tables): events/sessions capture, the knowledge-graph entities/relationships/knowledge_nodes, per-source enrichment queues (shell, claude, browser, workflow, agentic), workflow/CI tables, lessons, and capture-health/alarm tables.
nodes.languageNotes: Embedded via include_str! into storage.rs; uses sqlite-vec (vec0) virtual tables and FTS5 plus per-table indexes for the hybrid-search query path.

nodes.name: sessions
nodes.summary: Shell session records grouping captured events by host, shell, and user.

nodes.name: events
nodes.summary: Primary captured-event table (shell/browser/agentic) with timestamps, cwd, git context, source_kind, and enrichment state.

nodes.name: entities
nodes.summary: Knowledge-graph entities (people, repos, files, tools) with type, name, canonical form, and first/last-seen timestamps.

nodes.name: relationships
nodes.summary: Directed edges between entities forming the knowledge graph.

nodes.name: knowledge_nodes
nodes.summary: LLM-enriched knowledge nodes (summaries/lessons) embedded for sqlite-vec + FTS5 hybrid retrieval.

nodes.name: claude_sessions
nodes.summary: Ingested Claude Code / Codex coding-assistant session segments keyed by session id and segment index.

nodes.name: browser_events
nodes.summary: Captured browser activity (url, title, domain, dwell, scroll depth, extracted text) from the Firefox extension.

nodes.name: agentic_sessions
nodes.summary: Unified agentic coding-assistant session table keyed by harness, cwd, and start time.

nodes.name: workflow_runs
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow run records keyed by commit SHA and repository.

nodes.name: source_health
nodes.summary: Single SQL ground-truth row per capture source recording whether the last event landed, used by the watchdog invariants.

nodes.name: capture_alarms
nodes.summary: Watchdog-raised alarm rows recording capture-invariant violations, rate-limited per invariant.

nodes.name: lessons
nodes.summary: Clustered post-mortem lessons derived from workflow failures, scoped per repository.

nodes.name: schema_v4.sql
nodes.summary: Frozen v4 SQLite schema snapshot (16 tables, pre-workflow) used as the seed fixture for the schema_v5 migration test.

nodes.name: schema_v5.sql
nodes.summary: Frozen v5 SQLite schema snapshot (26 tables, adds workflow/lessons) used as the seed fixture for the schema_v6 migration test.

nodes.name: schema_v6.sql
nodes.summary: Frozen v6 SQLite schema snapshot used as the seed fixture for the schema_v7 migration test (which verifies the source_kind/tool_name additions).

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo manifest for the hippo-daemon crate: declares the `hippo` binary and `hippo_daemon` library, depends on hippo-core, and gates OpenTelemetry support behind a default `otel` feature.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses workspace-inherited versions and an optional-dependency feature group (`otel`) so telemetry crates only compile when the feature is enabled.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Developer README for the hippo-daemon crate: how to run the daemon (foreground or launchd), a table of all CLI commands, and a map of the crate's internal modules.

nodes.name: build.rs
nodes.summary: Cargo build script that derives the daemon's version string from git describe and exposes it to the crate via a compile-time env var.
nodes.languageNotes: Rust build.rs runs at compile time; emits cargo:rustc-env directives.

nodes.name: git_describe_version
nodes.summary: Runs git describe to compute a version string (tag + commit offset + dirty flag), falling back to the base version when git is unavailable.

nodes.name: backfill.rs
nodes.summary: One-shot batch backfill of Claude session JSONL files: resolves a glob, resets per-file ingest offsets, and re-runs segment extraction so historical sessions are enriched.

nodes.name: BackfillSummary
nodes.summary: Result record tallying files matched/processed/errored and segments updated/unchanged for a backfill run.

nodes.name: run_backfill
nodes.summary: Drives the backfill: collects matching JSONL paths since a cutoff, resets offsets, re-ingests each file, and aggregates a BackfillSummary.

nodes.name: collect_paths
nodes.summary: Expands a glob pattern (with tilde support) and filters Claude session files modified since an optional cutoff date.

nodes.name: parse_since_date
nodes.summary: Parses a --since date argument into Unix epoch milliseconds for backfill filtering.

nodes.name: expand_tilde
nodes.summary: Expands a leading tilde in a glob pattern to the user's home directory.

nodes.name: claude_session.rs
nodes.summary: Core Claude Code session parser: turns ~/.claude/projects JSONL transcripts into task-boundary SessionSegments, computes content hashes, and idempotently upserts segments plus enrichment-queue rows into SQLite.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of serde_json Value walking and content-hash-based idempotent INSERT OR IGNORE / enqueue decisions.

nodes.name: SessionSegment
nodes.summary: Parsed task-boundary segment of a Claude session: prompts, assistant texts, tool calls, timing, token count, and subagent lineage.

nodes.name: PendingToolUse
nodes.summary: In-flight tool-use record awaiting its matching tool_result, carrying session/cwd/git context for envelope assembly.

nodes.name: ToolCallSummary
nodes.summary: Compact (name, summary) pair describing a single tool invocation within a segment.

nodes.name: SessionFile
nodes.summary: Metadata for a discovered session JSONL: path, project dir, session id, and subagent lineage.

nodes.name: extract_segments
nodes.summary: Parses a full session JSONL into ordered SessionSegments, splitting on task boundaries and applying secret redaction.

nodes.name: insert_segments
nodes.summary: Idempotently upserts session segments into SQLite (INSERT OR IGNORE on session_id+segment_index) and decides per-segment enrichment-queue enqueue.

nodes.name: process_line
nodes.summary: Processes one JSONL line, pairing tool_use with tool_result and emitting a redacted Claude tool-call envelope for the daemon.

nodes.name: build_envelope
nodes.summary: Assembles a Claude tool-call event envelope from a pending tool use plus its result content and git/host context.

nodes.name: ingest_batch
nodes.summary: One-shot batch ingest of a single session file: extracts segments and sends them to the daemon over the Unix socket with a timeout.

nodes.name: format_tool_command
nodes.summary: Renders a human-readable command string for a tool invocation based on tool name and JSON input.

nodes.name: extract_tool_summary
nodes.summary: Extracts a concise summary for a tool-use content block for inclusion in a segment.

nodes.name: from_path
nodes.summary: Builds a SessionFile from a JSONL path, inferring project dir, session id, and subagent/parent lineage.

nodes.name: extract_user_text
nodes.summary: Extracts user prompt text from a Claude message, handling string and structured content variants.

nodes.name: extract_assistant_content
nodes.summary: Extracts assistant-authored text from a Claude message's content blocks.

nodes.name: build_summary_text
nodes.summary: Builds the human-readable summary text for a segment from its prompts and tool calls.

nodes.name: decide_enqueue
nodes.summary: Decides whether a segment should be (re)enqueued for enrichment based on insert status, content hash, and prior queue state.

nodes.name: compute_segment_content_hash
nodes.summary: Computes a stable content hash of a segment used to detect genuine changes and gate re-enqueue.

nodes.name: ingest_session_file
nodes.summary: Reads a session file and writes its segments directly to SQLite for one-shot ingestion.

nodes.name: write_session_segments
nodes.summary: Opens the database and persists extracted segments for a single session path.

nodes.name: cli.rs
nodes.summary: Clap-derived command-line interface definition for the hippo binary: the top-level Cli struct and the full Commands enum plus all subcommand action enums.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure clap derive structs/enums; no runtime logic, only the CLI surface.

nodes.name: Commands
nodes.summary: Top-level subcommand enum enumerating every hippo CLI verb (daemon, brain, status, ask, doctor, watchdog, probe, codex-poll, serve, etc.).

nodes.name: Cli
nodes.summary: Root clap parser struct holding the chosen top-level command.

nodes.name: DaemonAction
nodes.summary: Subcommands for the daemon verb: run, start, stop, restart, install, install-omlx.

nodes.name: SendEventSource
nodes.summary: Event-source variants (Shell, Watchlist) and their fields for the send-event CLI path.

nodes.name: codex_session.rs
nodes.summary: Codex (OpenAI) rollout ingestion: polls ~/.codex session roots, parses rollout-*.jsonl into task-boundary CodexSegments, upserts them into the shared claude_sessions table, and tracks coverage against Codex's own state DB.
nodes.languageNotes: Inode-keyed cursor in agentic_cursor survives Codex's archival mv; shares claude_enrichment_queue with Claude.

nodes.name: CodexSegment
nodes.summary: Parsed task-boundary segment of a Codex rollout: prompts, assistant texts, tool calls, timing, and source file.

nodes.name: CodexCoverageReport
nodes.summary: Coverage tally comparing Codex state-DB threads against ingested hippo sessions and in-flight rollouts.

nodes.name: ToolCall
nodes.summary: Compact (name, summary) record for a single Codex tool invocation.

nodes.name: CodexStateThread
nodes.summary: Row from Codex's state DB mapping a thread id to its rollout file path.

nodes.name: extract_segments
nodes.summary: Parses a Codex rollout JSONL into ordered CodexSegments, splitting on task boundaries and applying redaction.

nodes.name: poll_tick
nodes.summary: Single Codex poll cycle: walks session roots, skips in-flight/unchanged files via inode cursor, extracts segments, upserts them, and re-enqueues changed content.

nodes.name: upsert_segment_tx
nodes.summary: Upserts one Codex segment within a transaction via ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE, re-enqueuing only when content genuinely changed.

nodes.name: check_codex_coverage
nodes.summary: Cross-references Codex's state and log DBs against hippo's ingested sessions to produce a CodexCoverageReport.

nodes.name: extract_user_text
nodes.summary: Extracts user prompt text from a Codex rollout message payload.

nodes.name: tool_summary
nodes.summary: Builds a short summary string for a Codex tool call from its arguments JSON.

nodes.name: build_summary_text
nodes.summary: Assembles the human-readable summary text for a Codex segment.

nodes.name: decide_enqueue
nodes.summary: Decides whether a Codex segment should be re-enqueued for enrichment based on hash and prior queue state.

nodes.name: write_cursor
nodes.summary: Persists the inode-keyed ingest cursor (mtime + session id) so unchanged rollouts are skipped on the next tick.

nodes.name: content_text
nodes.summary: Flattens a Codex content value into plain text across its block variants.

nodes.name: is_file_in_idle_window
nodes.summary: Returns whether a rollout file was modified within the min-idle window, so in-flight files are skipped.

nodes.name: record_error
nodes.summary: Records a Codex ingestion error into source_health for watchdog/doctor visibility.

nodes.name: commands.rs
nodes.summary: CLI command handlers for the hippo binary: socket request/response helpers, send-event/status/sessions/events/query/alarms handlers, and the large `hippo doctor` health-check suite with its per-source freshness and staleness classifiers.
nodes.languageNotes: The doctor suite is built from many small isolated check_* functions, each returning a severity verdict; staleness suppression encodes per-source signals.

nodes.name: SocketProbeResult
nodes.summary: Outcome of probing the daemon Unix socket: Missing, Responsive, Stale, or Unresponsive.

nodes.name: AlarmRow
nodes.summary: A capture_alarms row as read for the alarms CLI: id, invariant, raised/resolved timestamps, and details JSON.

nodes.name: SuppressionSignals
nodes.summary: Per-source liveness signals (probe ok, firefox running, recent sessions, codex/opencode activity) used to suppress false staleness alarms in doctor.

nodes.name: SourceFreshnessProbe
nodes.summary: A named SQL freshness probe plus its soft/hard thresholds for a single capture source.

nodes.name: handle_doctor
nodes.summary: Runs the full `hippo doctor` suite: ten-plus isolated health checks with OK/WW/!!/-- severities, returning the failure count as exit code and optionally explaining each failure.

nodes.name: check_source_staleness
nodes.summary: Per-source staleness check that classifies each capture source's age against thresholds and suppresses false positives using liveness signals.

nodes.name: check_nm_manifest
nodes.summary: Doctor check validating the Firefox native-messaging manifest exists, points at the right binary, and is well-formed.

nodes.name: print_brain_health_details
nodes.summary: Queries the brain's health endpoint and prints detailed status (schema, models, telemetry, queue depths) for status/doctor output.

nodes.name: check_zsh_hook_sourced
nodes.summary: Doctor check verifying the zsh capture hook is sourced in the user's shell rc and matches the installed repo path.

nodes.name: check_claude_session_db
nodes.summary: Doctor check comparing active Claude session JSONLs on disk against ingested rows to detect ingestion gaps.

nodes.name: handle_send_event_shell
nodes.summary: Handles the shell send-event CLI path: builds a redacted shell event envelope and fires it to the daemon, with a fallback file on socket failure.

nodes.name: check_codex_state_coverage
nodes.summary: Doctor check that reports Codex ingestion coverage by comparing Codex's state DB against hippo's sessions.

nodes.name: check_session_hook_log
nodes.summary: Doctor check confirming the Claude SessionStart hook has been invoked recently by inspecting its debug log.

nodes.name: check_claude_session_hook_at
nodes.summary: Doctor check verifying the Claude session hook entry in settings.json matches the expected installed path.

nodes.name: check_log_file_sizes
nodes.summary: Doctor check warning when daemon/brain log files exceed size thresholds.

nodes.name: check_fallback_age
nodes.summary: Doctor check warning when fallback (socket-failure) event files are accumulating and aging, indicating the daemon is unreachable.

nodes.name: check_github_source_with
nodes.summary: Doctor check for the GitHub CI source: validates token presence and recent gh-poll activity.

nodes.name: check_watchdog_heartbeat
nodes.summary: Doctor check asserting the watchdog has written a recent heartbeat row to source_health.

nodes.name: check_schema_version
nodes.summary: Doctor check comparing the database schema version against what the brain expects, flagging mismatches.

nodes.name: check_otel_status
nodes.summary: Doctor check reporting OpenTelemetry export status for daemon and brain.

nodes.name: handle_alarms_list
nodes.summary: Lists unacknowledged capture alarms in a table, exiting non-zero when any are unresolved.

nodes.name: handle_sessions
nodes.summary: Handles the sessions CLI query: requests session summaries from the daemon and renders them.

nodes.name: handle_events
nodes.summary: Handles the events CLI query with session/since/project filters via the daemon socket.

nodes.name: handle_status
nodes.summary: Handles the status CLI command: probes the daemon and prints process/capture status.

nodes.name: probe_socket
nodes.summary: Probes the daemon Unix socket within a timeout and classifies it as Missing/Responsive/Stale/Unresponsive.

nodes.name: source_freshness_probes
nodes.summary: Returns the set of named SQL freshness probes and thresholds, one per capture source.

nodes.name: source_freshness_verdict
nodes.summary: Classifies a source's row count and max timestamp against thresholds into an OK/warn/fail verdict.

nodes.name: send_request_with_timeout
nodes.summary: Sends a length-prefixed JSON request to the daemon socket and reads the response under a timeout.

nodes.name: send_event_fire_and_forget
nodes.summary: Fire-and-forget send of an event envelope to the daemon socket, ignoring the response.

nodes.name: handle_alarms_ack
nodes.summary: Acknowledges a capture alarm by id, optionally attaching a note.

nodes.name: print_alarms_table
nodes.summary: Renders capture alarms as a formatted table, optionally including resolved rows.

nodes.name: daemon.rs

nodes.name: DaemonState

nodes.name: RollingCounts

nodes.name: run_with_mode

nodes.name: flush_events
nodes.summary: Drains the in-memory event buffer and batch-writes shell/tool/session/browser events plus source_health updates to SQLite in transactions.

nodes.name: handle_request

nodes.name: recompute_rolling_counts

nodes.name: handle_connection

nodes.name: framing.rs
nodes.summary: Length-prefixed framing helpers for the daemon's Unix-socket protocol: read and write a u32-length-prefixed JSON frame over an async stream.

nodes.name: read_frame
nodes.summary: Reads a u32 length prefix then that many bytes from the stream, returning the frame payload.

nodes.name: write_frame
nodes.summary: Writes a u32 length prefix followed by the payload bytes to the stream.

nodes.name: gh_api.rs

nodes.name: strokeSpiral

nodes.name: release-gui.sh

nodes.name: emit_json
nodes.summary: Serializes the release artifact metadata (paths, version, checksum, signature, verify command) to indented JSON via an embedded python3 heredoc.

nodes.name: run-native-app.sh
nodes.summary: Bash convenience script that builds the HippoGUI bundle via build-native-app.sh and opens the resulting .app.

nodes.name: setup-codex-mcp.sh
nodes.summary: Idempotent Bash script that registers the local xcode-proxy MCP HTTP endpoint with the codex CLI, skipping if already present.

nodes.name: stamp-app-version.sh
nodes.summary: Bash script that resolves the marketing version (from env, VERSION file, or Cargo.toml) and a build number (env or git commit count) and stamps CFBundleShortVersionString/CFBundleVersion into a target Info.plist via PlistBuddy.

nodes.name: resolve_marketing_version
nodes.summary: Resolves the marketing version by preferring HIPPO_MARKETING_VERSION, then the GUI VERSION file, then the workspace.package version parsed from Cargo.toml via awk.

nodes.name: resolve_build_number
nodes.summary: Resolves the build number from HIPPO_BUILD_NUMBER, BUILD_NUMBER, the git commit count, or a fallback of 1.

nodes.name: xcode-mcp-proxy.sh
nodes.summary: Idempotent Bash script that launches mcp-proxy wrapping `xcrun mcpbridge` on port 9876 so multiple agents share one long-lived Xcode MCP connection.

nodes.name: com.hippo.brain.plist
nodes.summary: LaunchAgent that runs the Python brain enrichment/query server (uv run hippo-brain serve) with KeepAlive and RunAtLoad, wiring OTel env vars and log paths via install-time placeholder substitution.
nodes.languageNotes: Placeholders like __UV_BIN__/__BRAIN_DIR__/__DATA_DIR__ are substituted by the installer at deploy time.

nodes.name: com.hippo.claude-session-watcher.plist
nodes.summary: LaunchAgent that runs the daemon's `claude-session-watch` FSEvents watcher with KeepAlive, RunAtLoad, and a 10s restart throttle to continuously ingest Claude session JSONL files.

nodes.name: com.hippo.codex-session.plist
nodes.summary: LaunchAgent that runs the daemon's `codex-poll` ingestion on a StartInterval cadence with WatchPaths on the Codex session directories to import Codex rollout files.

nodes.name: com.hippo.daemon.plist
nodes.summary: LaunchAgent that runs the Rust capture daemon (`hippo daemon run`) with KeepAlive and RunAtLoad, wiring OTel env vars and rolling log paths via install-time placeholder substitution.

nodes.name: com.hippo.gh-poll.plist
nodes.summary: LaunchAgent that invokes the install-generated gh-poll-wrapper.sh every 300 seconds to poll GitHub activity, with RunAtLoad disabled.

nodes.name: com.hippo.omlx.plist
nodes.summary: LaunchAgent that runs the local oMLX OpenAI-compatible inference server (`omlx serve` on 127.0.0.1:8000) with KeepAlive so the brain always has a model endpoint to call.
nodes.languageNotes: Placeholder tokens (__HOME__, __PATH__, __DATA_DIR__) are substituted by the `mise run install` task at install time.

nodes.name: com.hippo.opencode-poll.plist
nodes.summary: LaunchAgent that periodically invokes `hippo opencode-poll` (StartInterval-driven) to ingest OpenCode session rollouts into the capture pipeline.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses StartInterval (templated __OPENCODE_POLL_INTERVAL_SECS__) with RunAtLoad=false to avoid an install-time poll storm.

nodes.name: com.hippo.probe.plist
nodes.summary: LaunchAgent that runs `hippo probe` every 5 minutes, injecting synthetic canary events that round-trip through each capture path to measure end-to-end latency.
nodes.languageNotes: RunAtLoad=false prevents canary storms at bootstrap; probe rows carry a probe_tag and are filtered out of all user-facing queries.

nodes.name: com.hippo.watchdog.plist
nodes.summary: LaunchAgent that runs `hippo watchdog run` every 60 seconds to assert the I-1..I-12 capture invariants against source_health and raise capture_alarms on violations.

nodes.name: com.hippo.xcode-claude-ingest.plist
nodes.summary: LaunchAgent that ingests Xcode CodingAssistant Claude sessions by running the hippo-ingest-claude.py script over the Xcode projects directory, combining WatchPaths (push on new projects) with a 5-minute StartInterval poll.
nodes.languageNotes: WatchPaths only fires on the projects-root mtime change (new project subdir), so StartInterval provides the steady-state poll for appends inside existing project dirs.

nodes.name: mise.toml
nodes.summary: The project's mise task runner manifest defining ~70 tasks for building, testing, linting, formatting, installing, running, and operating every component (Rust daemon, Python brain, Swift GUI, browser extension, OTel stack).
nodes.languageNotes: Tasks embed inline bash scripts; the `install` task (lines 546+) orchestrates the full clean-install flow including launchd plist templating and verification.

nodes.name: README.md

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Docker Compose stack (project `hippo-otel`) wiring together the five observability services — OTel Collector, Tempo, Loki, Prometheus, and Grafana — with mounted configs and host-persisted data volumes.
nodes.languageNotes: Data volumes are mounted from ${HOME}/.local/share/hippo/otel/* so telemetry persists across container restarts; Grafana enables anonymous Admin access for local-only use.

nodes.name: loki

nodes.name: prometheus
nodes.summary: Prometheus service scraping and storing metrics on port 9090, with configurable TSDB retention by time and size via environment variables.

nodes.name: grafana
nodes.summary: Grafana service (port 3030) provisioned with the Hippo datasources and dashboards directories; depends on tempo, loki, and prometheus.

nodes.name: bench-model-comparison.json

nodes.name: bench-model-drilldown.json

nodes.name: hippo-enrichment.json

nodes.name: hippo-bulk-enrich.py
nodes.summary: Standalone CLI script that bulk-enriches pending shell events: it claims pending events grouped by session, builds enrichment prompts, calls the inference server, writes knowledge nodes, and embeds them into the vector store.
nodes.languageNotes: Reads model/storage settings directly from ~/.config/hippo/config.toml via tomllib and opens the SQLite DB with WAL/busy_timeout pragmas matching the daemon's conventions.

nodes.name: _parse_args
nodes.summary: Builds and parses the argparse CLI arguments controlling batch size, model override, and limits for the bulk enrichment run.

nodes.name: main

nodes.name: hippo-ingest-claude.py
nodes.summary: Standalone CLI script that batch-imports Claude session JSONL files into the claude_sessions table (extracting task-boundary segments) and optionally runs enrichment of the resulting pending segments into knowledge nodes.
nodes.languageNotes: Invoked by the xcode-claude-ingest LaunchAgent with --claude-dir; the enrichment phase is async (asyncio.run(run_enrichment(...))).

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point that parses CLI args, resolves config and DB paths, iterates Claude session files extracting and inserting segments, then conditionally kicks off async enrichment of any pending segments.

nodes.name: run_enrichment
nodes.summary: Async routine that claims pending Claude segments, calls the inference client to enrich them, writes Claude knowledge nodes, and embeds the results into the vector store.

nodes.name: hippo-re-embed.py
nodes.summary: Standalone CLI script that rebuilds the entire vector index by re-embedding every knowledge_node with the currently configured embedding model after dropping the old LanceDB vectors directory.
nodes.languageNotes: Takes no arguments by design — it always re-embeds all nodes using models.embedding from config.toml, padding/truncating vectors to the table dimension.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point that loads the embedding-model config, drops the existing vectors directory, recreates the LanceDB table, and re-embeds every knowledge_node into it.

nodes.name: hippo-monitor.sh
nodes.summary: Live terminal monitor that polls the hippo SQLite DB on an interval and renders enrichment queue depths, a progress bar, knowledge-store counts, and the most recent enrichments.
nodes.languageNotes: Aggregates the three enrichment queues (shell, claude, browser) in one SQL UNION ALL and shells out to `uv run` for the LanceDB vector count.

nodes.name: install.sh

nodes.languageNotes: Receipt-based idempotency: each component records its installed checksum so re-running skips unchanged artifacts unless HIPPO_FORCE is set.

nodes.name: detect_platform

nodes.name: download_file

nodes.name: verify_checksum
nodes.summary: Verifies a downloaded artifact's SHA256 against the release's SHA256SUMS.txt before installation.

nodes.name: parse_checksum
nodes.summary: Extracts the expected SHA256 hash for a given asset name out of the release checksum manifest.

nodes.name: check_receipt
nodes.summary: Reads a component's install receipt and reports whether the installed checksum already matches, enabling idempotent skips.

nodes.name: write_receipt
nodes.summary: Records a per-component install receipt (checksum + version) under the XDG state directory after a successful install.

nodes.name: install_daemon

nodes.name: verify_brain_imports

nodes.name: setup_config
nodes.summary: Seeds the default config under ~/.config/hippo when no user configuration is present yet.

nodes.name: install_services
nodes.summary: Registers and starts the Hippo launchd services after the binaries and config are in place.

nodes.name: verify_installation
nodes.summary: Runs a battery of post-install checks (binary on PATH, brain imports, services running) to confirm a healthy installation.

nodes.name: check_dependencies
nodes.summary: Verifies required host tools (bash, curl, uv, python3) are available before proceeding with installation.

nodes.name: warn_on_stale_shell_hook_sources
nodes.summary: Warns the user when their shell rc files still source outdated Hippo hook paths after an install.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full install sequence: dependency checks, platform detection, release resolution, component installs, config setup, service start, and verification.

nodes.summary: Selects the next non-blocked, incomplete task from the Ralph state file to feed into the next claude iteration.

nodes.name: check-shell-secrets.sh

nodes.name: require_cmd

nodes.name: print_matches

nodes.name: silently_drop_via_arbitrary_name

nodes.name: explicit_error_handling

tour.title: What Hippo Is
tour.description: Start here to understand the whole system before any code. The README lays out hippo's pitch: a local-first 'second brain' where a Rust daemon captures your shell, Claude Code, and browser activity over a Unix socket and a Python brain enriches it into a searchable knowledge graph. CLAUDE.md is the engineering companion to that pitch, naming the two processes, the shared SQLite database, and the XDG path conventions every later step depends on. Read these two and you have the map for the rest of the tour.

tour.title: The Daemon Entry Point
tour.description: This is where the running system begins. main.rs is the binary entry point: it parses the CLI with clap, initializes telemetry, and dispatches every `hippo` subcommand (daemon, install, doctor, watchdog, probe, the session pollers) to its handler. cli.rs is the command tree it parses, so reading these two together shows you the complete surface area of the tool — capture, query, install, and operate — all from one binary.
tour.languageLesson: Rust binaries built with clap's derive API turn a struct/enum of subcommands into a parser at compile time. main.rs is thin by design — it matches on the parsed enum and delegates, keeping the entry point a routing table rather than a place where logic accumulates.

tour.title: The Long-Lived Capture Server
tour.description: Now follow what `hippo daemon` actually starts. daemon.rs is the persistent process that listens on the Unix socket, accepts two kinds of traffic — fire-and-forget capture events and request/response CLI queries — and buffers events before batch-flushing them into SQLite. This is the hub every capture source on the next two steps writes into, and the same socket the CLI reads back out of.
tour.languageLesson: The shell hook must never block, so the daemon treats capture as fire-and-forget: it acknowledges the socket write immediately and batches inserts on its own schedule. Separating the hot ingest path from the durable write is a recurring reliability theme in this codebase (see the anti-patterns in Step 11).

tour.title: Capture Source 1 — The Shell
tour.description: With the server understood, meet the first thing that feeds it. hippo.zsh is the zsh hook that wraps every command via preexec/precmd, recording the command text, cwd, git state, timing, and exit code, then firing it at the daemon socket. claude-session-hook.sh is a deliberately tiny no-op SessionStart hook whose only job is to leave a log line that `hippo doctor` can verify — the real Claude ingestion is a filesystem watcher, not this hook. Together they show hippo's lightest-touch capture path.

tour.title: Capture Sources 2 & 3 — Agent Sessions
tour.description: Hippo's most distinctive capture comes from coding-assistant transcripts. watch_claude_sessions.rs is an FSEvents watcher that tails `~/.claude/projects/**/*.jsonl`, re-extracts conversation segments on every file growth, and inserts them idempotently. codex_session.rs and opencode_session.rs do the same idea for other agents on a polling cadence, sharing the same enrichment queue. This is what lets hippo later link a shell command to the exact AI conversation that produced it.
tour.languageLesson: Idempotent ingestion is the trick that makes file-watching safe: segments are inserted with `INSERT OR IGNORE` / `ON CONFLICT (session_id, segment_index)`, so re-processing a file that merely grew updates or skips rows instead of duplicating them. Per-file offsets/cursors track resume position across restarts.

tour.title: Capture Source 4 — The Browser Bridge
tour.description: The fourth source crosses a process boundary. The Firefox extension's content.ts tracks dwell time and scroll depth on allowlisted pages and extracts main article text via Readability; background.ts relays those page_visit messages to the daemon over Native Messaging. native_messaging.rs is the daemon's stdin/stdout end of that bridge, invoked by Firefox itself. manifest.json declares the permissions and the allowlist that scope what the extension may ever see.
tour.languageLesson: Native Messaging is how a browser extension talks to a local binary: the browser launches the host process and exchanges length-prefixed JSON over stdio. The Rust side mirrors the same length-prefixed framing the daemon uses on its Unix socket (next step), so the wire discipline is consistent across transports.

tour.title: The Shared Wire Format
tour.description: Every source above speaks one vocabulary, defined in the shared hippo-core crate. events.rs declares the EventEnvelope and its EventPayload variants (Shell, Browser, AgenticToolCall, FsChange, Raw) — the capture wire format. protocol.rs defines the request/response enums the CLI and daemon exchange, and framing.rs is the length-prefixed JSON transport underneath both the socket and Native Messaging. lib.rs is the crate root that re-exports it all to both the daemon and the Python brain's expectations.
tour.languageLesson: Rust enums are sum types: EventPayload as a tagged enum means a single value is exactly one of Shell/Browser/AgenticToolCall/etc., and serde serializes the tag alongside the data. Pattern-matching on that enum is exhaustive, so adding a new capture source forces every consumer to handle it — the compiler enforces the contract.

tour.title: Redaction & Configuration
tour.description: Before any captured text is stored, secrets are scrubbed — redaction is first-class here, not an afterthought. redaction.rs compiles the configured patterns into a RegexSet and replaces matches before the write, returning per-pattern hit counts. config.rs defines the entire configuration tree (inference, models, daemon, brain, storage, browser, telemetry, watchdog, codex, reaper) with serde defaults, and the two default templates show what ships out of the box and which secret patterns are scrubbed on day one.
tour.languageLesson: A RegexSet compiles many patterns into one automaton and tests them in a single pass, which is why redaction can run on the capture path without becoming a bottleneck. Models and inference endpoints are config-driven (never hardcoded), so hippo stays vendor-neutral about which local LLM it talks to.

tour.title: Storage & the Data Model
tour.description: This is the contract both processes share: a single SQLite database. storage.rs opens the connection, embeds and migrates schema.sql, and exposes the read/write functions for sessions, events, and entities. schema.sql is the canonical schema (34 tables); the tables highlighted here are the spine of the system — raw `events`, the per-source `*_sessions` and `browser_events`, the LLM-enriched `knowledge_nodes` that retrieval searches, and `source_health`, the one-row-per-source ground truth that the reliability stack in Step 11 is built on.
tour.languageLesson: SQLite in WAL mode lets the Rust daemon write while the Python brain reads concurrently, with `busy_timeout` smoothing contention — the schema is the integration boundary between the two languages, so there is no RPC layer between them, just shared rows. Migrations embed the schema and advance a version pragma so both processes agree on shape.

tour.title: The Brain & Enrichment Pipeline
tour.description: Cross the language boundary into Python. server.py is the brain's main process: a Starlette HTTP app plus the async orchestrator that polls every source's enrichment queue, calls the local LLM with retries, and writes knowledge nodes. enrichment.py holds the core logic — eligibility filtering, prompt construction, response parsing, and entity/knowledge-node writes — and client.py is the vendor-neutral HTTP client for any OpenAI-compatible inference server (with a deterministic mock for tests). This cluster is the most depended-upon code in the whole project.
tour.languageLesson: The orchestrator enriches shell, Claude, and browser sources concurrently via asyncio.gather, and kicks embeddings off as background tasks so LLM inference and vector writes overlap. Async here is about overlapping I/O-bound LLM calls, not CPU parallelism — the event loop keeps many in-flight requests cheap.

tour.title: Embeddings & the Vector Store
tour.description: Enriched knowledge is only useful if it's retrievable, which means vectors. embeddings.py orchestrates embedding each knowledge node — opening the vector DB, checking dimensions, padding or truncating to fit — and runs similarity search. vector_store.py is the low-level layer beneath it: sqlite-vec (vec0) virtual tables plus FTS5, opened with the same WAL/foreign-key pragmas as the rest of the DB, with an explicit guard against silently mixing embeddings from different models. This is the substrate the next step's hybrid retrieval queries.
tour.languageLesson: sqlite-vec keeps vector search inside the same SQLite file as the relational data, so semantic and lexical (FTS5) indexes live side by side — no separate vector database to operate. The model-drift guard matters because embeddings from two different models are not comparable; mixing them silently corrupts similarity results.

tour.title: Querying — RAG, Retrieval & MCP
tour.description: This is the payoff: turning the knowledge graph back into answers. retrieval.py is the hybrid engine — semantic, lexical, recent, and combined search modes with MMR diversification over sqlite-vec + FTS5. rag.py wraps it for `hippo ask`: it retrieves and shapes hits, budgets a synthesis prompt under a payload cap, calls the LLM, and returns an answer with scored sources. mcp.py exposes the same retrieval and RAG layers as MCP tools (ask, search_knowledge, search_events, get_entities), making hippo natively usable from any MCP client.
tour.languageLesson: Hybrid retrieval fuses dense (vector) and sparse (FTS5 keyword) results, then MMR re-ranks for relevance-vs-redundancy so the prompt isn't filled with near-duplicate hits. RAG = retrieve-then-synthesize: the LLM only sees a budgeted set of grounded sources, and the answer ships with citations rather than free-floating claims.

tour.title: Capture-Reliability Stack
tour.description: A capture system is only as good as your confidence that events actually landed — this is the subsystem that earns that trust. watchdog.rs reads source_health every 60s and asserts the I-1..I-14 invariants, writing rate-limited capture_alarms on violations and auto-resolving cleared ones. probe.rs sends synthetic canary events through each capture path and records their round-trip latency. commands.rs houses the `hippo doctor` health-check suite that classifies per-source freshness. The capture docs explain the whole design, with source_health (from Step 9) as the single SQL ground truth.
tour.languageLesson: The design rule is 'one SQL ground truth': every capture path writes its source_health row in the same transaction as the event insert, so the watchdog can reason about liveness from a single table instead of inferring it. Synthetic probes are tagged and filtered out of every user-facing query, so the canaries never pollute real recall.

tour.title: Surfaces & Operations
tour.description: Finish with how hippo is presented and kept running. The Swift GUI's HippoGUIApp.swift owns a BrainClient and a NavigationSplitView (Query/Knowledge/Events/Status), giving the daemon+brain a native macOS face, while the Astro site's index.astro is the public marketing/architecture page. Operationally, the launchd plists are what actually supervise each long-lived process — daemon, brain, the Claude watcher, watchdog, and probe — and otel/docker-compose.yml stands up the optional Grafana/Tempo/Loki/Prometheus observability stack. This closes the loop from code to a running, observable system.
tour.languageLesson: launchd is macOS's service supervisor: each plist declares a process with KeepAlive (restart on exit) or StartInterval (run on a cadence), which is why hippo's daemon stays up while the watchdog and probe fire periodically. The OTel stack is env-gated and entirely optional — instrumentation ships dormant and only activates when you opt in.
